run_status: not yet run -- this file is the PRE-REGISTRATION, written before any executor or grading call
question: Does a tiered configuration (sonnet plans + validates, haiku implements, <=2 worker iterations) match sonnet-solo quality at materially lower cost or time?

executor.vehicle: harness.py (Python stdlib), a hermetic claude -p driver run inside WSL Debian, replacing the original harness.workflow.js (Workflow tool) design -- see README 'Executor: harness.py (claude -p driver)' for why. grade.workflow.js and prosecute_counts.workflow.js are unaffected: they only ever handled small verdict records, not full response text or per-cell timing.
executor.hermeticity: claude -p hermetic per ADR 0023: clean CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR ($HOME/issue30/claude-config -- no plugins, credentials only), --disallowedTools tool deny list (Skill Task Read Grep Glob Bash Edit Write WebFetch WebSearch NotebookEdit) so no repo access, prompt supplied on stdin only, and every call run from an empty scratch cwd outside the repo checkout (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR does not stop project-level CLAUDE.md auto-discovery by cwd traversal -- verified empirically before shipping the driver).
executor.cost_time_capture: real per-cell claude -p --output-format json result envelope (usage, total_cost_usd, duration_ms, duration_api_ms) plus harness.py's own measured wall-clock per call -- not the Workflow runner's per-arm-aggregate-only budget.spent() compromise.
executor.parallelism: ThreadPoolExecutor with EXACTLY 4 workers, fixed identically for every arm (protocol symmetry) -- not exposed as a per-arm-tunable flag.
executor.effort: medium (CLAUDE_EFFORT=medium in the executor env, matching prior benchmarks)
executor.arm_symmetry_control: no skill content is injected in any arm; every arm gets the identical NEUTRAL_FRAME instruction not to read files, search the repo, run commands, or use any tools. ADR 0023's hermeticity requirement scopes to the control arm (ADR 0027 scoping note); harness.py's --disallowedTools now enforces tool denial directly (not merely a runner convention), and ARM SYMMETRY (identical constraints, identically worded, across all 3 arms) still applies on top of it.

tiered_design.pattern: orchestrator (sonnet) writes a brief plan/spec -> worker (haiku) implements from the spec -> orchestrator (sonnet) validates against the ORIGINAL request -> may redispatch the worker ONCE with corrections
tiered_design.final_deliverable_rule: always the worker's LAST output; the orchestrator never rewrites it (rewriting would contaminate which tier produced the graded deliverable)

grading.prosecutor: uniform adversarial re-count on ALL cells (ADR 0025); met_final = min(grader, prosecutor)
grading.grader_independence_residual: grader is sonnet, the same tier as the sonnet-solo arm and the tiered orchestrator. Grader independence is unsolvable across only 2 model tiers (issue #41 owner call, comment 2026-07-09); accepted as the same Claude-grades-Claude family-bias residual ADR 0019 already carries, not a new confound specific to this benchmark.

metric.headline: fraction_met (met/total), eval-clustered mean delta + 95% CI (ADR 0019, ADR 0025)
metric.secondary: binary_pass (all-expectations-met), not computed as a separate headline here (fraction-met is primary per ADR 0025); available by reusing grade.workflow.js's per-cell pass field if wanted later
metric.clustering_unit: one of the 24 evals -- reps average within an eval before clustering, per ADR 0019

cost_time_capture.design: Superseded the original Workflow-runner compromise (no Date.now() inside a workflow script; budget.spent() only gave a PER-ARM AGGREGATE token delta since pipeline() runs a full arm's 72 cells concurrently). harness.py's claude -p driver measures wall-clock directly per call (time.monotonic() bracketing each subprocess call) and parses claude -p --output-format json's real result envelope (usage, total_cost_usd, duration_ms, duration_api_ms) per call -- true per-cell figures, not an aggregate estimate. Fields are read defensively (json envelope shape may vary by CLI version); whatever usage object is present is stored verbatim per call alongside the measured wall-clock.
cost_time_capture.per_cell_record: <outdir>/cells/<arm>.<skill>.<eval_id>.r<rep>.json -- full per-call breakdown (plan/worker1/validate1/worker2 for tiered; exec for solo arms)
cost_time_capture.per_arm_summary: <outdir>/<arm>.summary.json -- {arm, cells, failures, total_tokens_in, total_tokens_out, total_cost_usd, wall_clock_s, per_call_parallelism}; aggregate.py reads these three files (costs.json and timing.json are deleted, along with their null-valued operator-filled-template convention)

sample_rule: keep 1 raw output per (skill, eval_id, arm) group, sorted-rep order; archive the rest into outputs/all.tar.gz via benchmarks/lib/bench_io.sample_and_archive_raw (ADR 0023 sample rule, ADR 0026 gzip-the-rest)

adoption_bar.primary: mean_delta(tiered - sonnet-solo) > -0.05 on fraction-met (non-inferiority margin, point estimate, eval-clustered over the 24 evals)
adoption_bar.secondary_confidence: the delta's 95% CI is reported, not gating -- strong confidence if its lower bound also clears -0.05, weak otherwise (ADR-0027-style directional bar: CI-exclusion of a -0.05 margin is not reliably achievable at n=24 clustered evals, same rationale ADR 0027 used for its diff bar)
adoption_bar.cost_gate: AND tokens_tiered <= 0.6 * tokens_sonnet-solo OR time_tiered <= 0.6 * time_sonnet-solo
adoption_bar.decision: ADOPT tiered only if BOTH the quality bar and the cost-or-time gate clear. Otherwise DO NOT ADOPT; record the result either way (ADR 0024, append-only).
adoption_bar.haiku_solo_role: reported vs sonnet-solo for context (the cost/quality floor) -- does not gate the tiered adoption decision
